text: Taking advantage of the isolation of the top two or three sympathetic gangliots of the hands and armpits, we can actually remove them and effectively create warm, dry hands instead of sweaty, cold and clammy hands.

So this is using the hook cautery to open up an envelope, open up a window in the pleural envelope over the nerve to dissect out the sympathetic chain from just above the second rib to just above the fourth rib.
Once we've opened the pleural envelope and created the window over the nerve, you can see here the actual nerve and the chain where it gets a little bigger is the sympathetic ganglia right there.
Once we've dissected out the chain from just above the second rib, right there, we put a clip at the top.
to just above the fourth rib.
There's the third rib going all the way down.
We then take a scissors to actually cut the nerve just above the bottom clip and just below the top clip.
so that we can remove the intervening segment of the nerve and then have people with warm, dry hands.
Again, this is done through two small incisions in the armpit.
